summary.intent: Fix the 'Waiting for first frame...' issue in a browser screencast feature where no frames were arriving for about:blank pages.
summary.outcome: Resolved by skipping auto-start of screencast on blank sessions and instead auto-starting it after the user navigates to a real URL; added a helpful canvas placeholder message for selected-but-not-running state.

repo: CDP (Chrome DevTools Protocol) screencast only sends frame events when a page has visual content — about:blank pages produce no frames.
Browser session management lives in crates/browser/src/screencast.rs and crates/web/src/assets/js/page-browser.js.

code.finding: navigateSession was updated to auto-start screencast after navigation if not already running; selectSession no longer auto-starts screencast for blank pages.

workflow: Changes were committed and pushed as a single atomic step after edits were complete.

friction: CDP screencast silently produces no frames for about:blank, which caused a misleading 'Waiting for first frame...' hang with no error feedback to the user.
open_items: The canvas placeholder message is a workaround — a more polished UX might show a 'Navigate to a URL to start viewing' prompt inline rather than relying on a placeholder state.
No automated tests added for the screencast blank-page edge case.
